note: The iconic colourful harbour; the boardwalk is mostly flat and accessible for enjoying the atmosphere, and canal boat tours have accessible boat options (book ahead). A must-see.

Historic amusement park with wide paved pathways and accessible restrooms; many rides need you to transfer from your chair, but rolling the magical gardens is worthwhile alone. Free carer tickets often available.
The statue sits just off the wide, well-paved, flat Langelinie Promenade - easy to view from the path, wheelchair/stroller friendly, and free. Small statue, big icon.

The 1600s castle interior is NOT wheelchair accessible - multiple floors via stairs, no lift, and staff may not permit taking a chair upstairs. BUT the King's Garden (Kongens Have) around it is fully accessible and lovely. Virtual interior tour online.
Home of the royal family; the square/courtyard is fully accessible for the daily noon Changing of the Guard, and the museum has elevator access. Flat and easy.
One of Europe's longest pedestrian shopping streets; mostly flat with ample space between shops and cafes. Some individual older shops have a step. Easy central stroll.
Excellent accessibility - ramps and elevators throughout the extensive exhibitions, all surfaces wheelchair accessible. Great rainy-day option for families.
Famous for its interior spiral RAMP (rare and wheelchair-friendly most of the way up), BUT the final section to the viewing platform is two sets of stairs, so the very top is not accessible.
Seat of parliament with grand halls; parts have access but the approach has some of the city's roughest cobblestones (brief but bumpy). Check specific accessible entrances.
Beautiful romantic park around Frederiksberg Palace with wide paved paths; relaxed, scenic and stroller-friendly. A calm accessible retreat.
